articles.title: IN PICS: Pedestrians cover their heads in the scorching heat of Mumbai - Mid-day
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Maharashtra
articles.full_text:
Mumbai and its suburbs on Thursday were likely to experience mainly clear skies along with hot and humid conditions, the India Meteorological Department (IMD) said
The IMD had forecast maximum and minimum temperatures to hover around 36 degrees Celsius and 23 degrees Celsius
Several parts of Maharashtra continued to witness rising temperatures, with multiple districts recording maximum temperatures close to 39 degrees Celsius, according to IMD's latest data
Maharashtra continued to witness warm conditions on Wednesday, with several districts recording above-normal temperatures, according to the IMD's latest weather report issued
In the Mumbai Metropolitan Region, Santacruz recorded 36.6 degrees Celsius, significantly above normal by 3.4 degrees, while Colaba reported a comparatively lower 34.2 degrees Celsius on Wednesday
Overall, the weather pattern indicates hot conditions in interior Maharashtra and humid weather along the coast, with temperatures in several areas remaining above normal for this time of year
People relax and play in the waves under the blazing sun as IMD forecasted temperature of 36 degree Celsius
India Meteorological Department on Tuesday issued yellow alert for Mumbai; hot, humid conditions expected, while Colaba saw slightly lower temperatures at 34.9 degrees Celsius
Meanwhile, the city’s air quality remained in the satisfactory category, with the Air Quality Index (AQI) recorded at 79 on Thursday.
